LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS DEFENDANT CHRIS CARNES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS REPLETE WITH FACTS THAT PROVIDE A BASIS FOR THE CLAIMS. CORP. CODE SECTION 16405 (A) AUTHORIZES THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FACTS SET FORTH IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT NEGATE THE CONTENTION THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT WAS A DEPENDENT ADULT WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE WELFARE & INSTITUTIONS CODE. ORDER SIGNED AFTER THE HEARING. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Brook Carroll (788.7600) for plaintiff/cross-defendant and moving party
Bryan Whipple (707.523.4497) for defendant/cross complainant and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT CHRIS CARNES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the 3rd and 4th causes of action is overruled. The cross-complaint is replete with facts that provide a basis for the claims. Corp. Code section 16405 (a) authorizes the breach of contract claim. Demurrer is sustained as to the 7th cause of action without leave to amend. The facts set forth in the cross-complaint negate the contention that cross-complainant was a dependent adult within the meaning of the Welfare & Institutions Code. Order signed after the hearing. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Chris Carnes' Motion To Strike Allegations In Defendant'S First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Brook Carroll (788.7600) for plaintiff/cross-defendant and moving party
Bryan Whipple (707.523.4497) for defendant/cross complainant and responding party

Argued and the Court ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT CHRIS CARNES' MOTION TO STRIKE ALLEGATIONS IN DEFENDANT'S FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is granted as to the treble damages claim and allegations regarding SSA disability. Order signed after the hearing. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
